schema.appDescription: Calculate additional interest cost from a loan moratorium or payment holiday. See how deferred EMIs increase your total debt.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Moratorium Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see how numbers evolve year by year.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option for you.

faq.q: What happens to interest during a loan moratorium?
faq.a: During a moratorium (payment holiday), EMIs are paused but interest continues to accrue on the outstanding principal. This deferred interest is added to the loan principal (capitalised), increasing your total loan amount. Post-moratorium EMIs are higher or tenure extends.

faq.q: Is moratorium the same as loan waiver?
faq.a: No. Moratorium is a temporary deferral — interest accrues and must be repaid later. A loan waiver permanently cancels the debt. Government moratoriums (like RBI's COVID-19 moratorium) were payment deferrals, not waivers. Customers who availed the RBI 2020 moratorium paid more total interest.

faq.q: When should I opt for a loan moratorium?
faq.a: Opt for moratorium only when facing genuine cash flow crisis (job loss, medical emergency). If you can pay EMIs, avoid moratorium — the additional interest cost is significant. A 3-month moratorium on a ₹20 lakh home loan at 8.5% adds approximately ₹43,000 in extra interest.
Yes, completely free with no registration on CalcPhi.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We never store your financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the Moratorium Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi works on all modern smartphones and tablets.

examples.heading: 3-month moratorium on ₹20 lakh home loan
examples.text: ₹20 lakh at 8.5% with 15 years remaining: Normal EMI = ₹19,720. After 3-month moratorium, deferred interest ≈ ₹43,000 added to principal. New EMI = ₹20,001 for remaining tenure. Total additional cost: ₹52,000.

examples.heading: COVID-19 moratorium impact — 6 months
examples.text: 6-month moratorium on ₹30 lakh loan at 8% with 20 years remaining: interest accrued = ₹1,20,000. This increases loan principal to ₹31.2 lakh, raising EMI by ₹800/month for 20 years — total additional cost ₹1.92 lakh.
